executive_summary:
The playtest session followed a high-stakes Honor Mode run focused on a Bard character. The player progressed through the Nautiloid, Act 1 exploration, and deep into Act 2, culminating in several boss confrontations. The session was defined by an extreme 'high-low' rhythm, where periods of strategic mastery and luck were interrupted by technical instability and character-build dissatisfaction.

Two critical issues stand out for immediate attention: recurring technical bugs and mechanical clarity gaps. Specifically, a total application crash (3:41) and a boss de-rendering glitch during the Grym encounter (18:51) represent severe stability risks. Additionally, the permanent loss of the Halsin questline due to lack of visual feedback on NPC status in Honor Mode (10:20) caused significant player frustration and a sense of 'locking out' content without warning.

Strengths to preserve include the deep satisfaction provided by class-specific narrative choices and non-combat resolution paths. The player expressed high delight when discovering unique Bard dialogue (3:53) and using charisma to bypass bosses (16:29), suggesting that the 'Bard power fantasy' of mental manipulation is a primary driver of engagement. The College of Swords power spike at Level 6 (13:26) also served as a major retention anchor after early subclass boredom.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 3:41, the game suffered a hard crash after using Vicious Mockery. At 18:51, the boss Grym de-rendered mid-fight after a lever interaction, leading the player to yell, 'Larian, fix your game please!'
key_findings.finding: Technical instability, including application crashes and boss model de-rendering, causes severe frustration and disrupts the flow of high-stakes play.
key_findings.recommended_action: Investigate Vicious Mockery VFX/logic on the Nautiloid and the central anvil collision/trigger logic in the Adamantine Forge.

key_findings.evidence_summary: At 10:20, the player discovers Halsin's non-revivable corpse and realizes they are 'locked out' of fixing the shadow-cursed lands. At 10:56, an 'Oath Broken' condition appears on Lae'zel without clear real-time warning during the goblin fight.
key_findings.finding: Irreversible narrative consequences in Honor Mode occur without sufficient visual feedback or warnings, leading to accidental quest failure.
key_findings.recommended_action: Implement a 'Warning: Tenet Violation' UI indicator and a clearer visual signifier (like Death Saving Throws) for quest-essential NPCs during large-scale brawls.

key_findings.evidence_summary: The player admitted they were 'not really having much fun' at 12:41 with the College of Glamour, but their sentiment score spiked to a 10/10 at 13:30 after respeccing to College of Swords and seeing the 'Extra Attack' feature.
key_findings.finding: The College of Glamour subclass lacks early-game tactical satisfaction compared to the Swords subclass, creating a boredom-based churn risk.
key_findings.recommended_action: Review early-game ability utility for College of Glamour to ensure it provides comparable active combat engagement to Swords.

key_findings.evidence_summary: The player hovered over the 'Ritual' tag at 4:30 and expressed shock that Longstrider didn't cost a slot despite 2000 hours played. At 14:13, the player expressed annoyance that the 'Club of Hill Giant Strength' gives 19 STR instead of the lore-accurate 21.
key_findings.finding: Inconsistent naming conventions and subtle UI tags (Rituals) result in even veteran players missing critical resource-saving mechanics.
key_findings.recommended_action: Rename the 'Club of Hill Giant Strength' to match its stat output and increase the visual prominence of the 'Ritual' tag in tooltips.

priority_actions: Debug crash trigger associated with Vicious Mockery in tutorial combat (3:41).
Fix model de-rendering/collision logic for Grym when the forge lever is toggled (18:51).
Add a confirmation prompt or visual warning when an action will permanently kill a quest-critical NPC like Halsin (10:20).
Revise item naming for 'Club of Hill Giant Strength' to ensure mechanical consistency with D&D lore expectations (14:13).
Implement a one-time tutorial pop-up when a player prepares their first 'Ritual' spell to explain its out-of-combat benefits (4:30).
cross_dimensional_insight: A 'Luck Buffer' phenomenon is evident: the player's high volume of Natural 20s (hitting 7 by 7:55) significantly mitigated the frustration of a near-party wipe at 7:32. This extreme RNG success generated enough positive sentiment to carry the player through a technical crash and early subclass boredom, suggesting that perceived 'luck' acts as a primary emotional shock absorber for technical and balance friction.
